title: Editorial: New Approaches to ­Research and Curatorial Practice
content:
Dr Kirsi Eskelinen, Director, Sinebrychoff Art Museum / Finnish National Gallery

June 17, 2026

This issue of FNG Research is rich in approaches to research into visual arts and curatorial practices. For example the Sinebrychoff Art Museum recently opened its themed exhibition, ‘Night’ which explores the night and the world of dreams, illuminated by old European art and works from the 19th century to the present. Besides the artworks, poetry also has a powerful presence in the exhibition. Curator Kersti Tainio decided to commission contemporary poet Henriikka Tavi to write poems specific to the themes in each room instead of using traditional wall texts. Rather than explaining this special time of day to visitors, they are encouraged to feel and interpret the night. Instead of a set of distinct works, the exhibition is a total work of art, with its architecture – devised by designer Lauri Johansson – also playing a major role. Gill Crabbe discusses the collaborative process with Henriikka Tavi in her article ‘A Night to Remember’.

Dr Kirsi Eskelinen, Director, Sinebrychoff Art Museum / Finnish National Gallery

June 17, 2026
creator: Kirsi Eskelinen
sourceDescription: FNG Research is the online magazine of the Finnish National Gallery. This online publication aims to promote the research activities of the Gallery, research being a part of its core functions. The Finnish National Gallery consists of three museums – the Ateneum Art Museum, the Museum of Contemporary Art Kiasma, the Sinebrychoff Art Museum – and the Department for Collections Management. FNG Research is a magazine for art-museum and art-history professionals internationally. The site is updated bi-monthly with new developments and content.
